content_en: Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi Masaabeeh al-Sunnah, al-Khateeb al-Baghdaadi in Tareekh Baghdaad, al­ Dailami in al-Firdaus, ibn Battah in al-lbaanah and Abu al-Qaasim al­ Asbahaani in al-Hujjah. 1 It was also recorded by al-Hasan ibn Sufyaan in al­ Arbaeen, al-Salafi in al-Arbaeen al-Buldaaniyah, al-Harawi in Dhamm al­ Kalaam and ibn Asaakir in al-Arbaeen.2 All of those scholars have recorded this hadith through the chain of Nuaim ibn Hammaad al-Khuzaa'ee. In most cases, it was Nuaim from Abdul Wahaab ibn Abdul Majeed al-Thaqafi from Hishaam ibn Hasaan from Muhammad ibn Sireen from Uqbah ibn Aus from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As. Except for Nuaim, all of those narrators are definitely acceptable narrators.3 For the most part, the grading of this hadith is determined by how one views Nuaim ibn Hammaad. Among the scholars who discussed Nuaim ibn Hammaad, it seems that ibn Hajr gives him the highest evaluation. In Tahdheeb al-Tahdheeb, ibn Hajr quotes most of what al-Mizi said about Nuaim in Tahdheeb al-Kamaal. Those quotes contain both favorable and unfavorable remarks. Then in his own discussion at the end, seemingly summarizing his views on Nuaim, ibn Hajr wrote, As for Nuaim, his righteousness and honesty is confirmed, but in his hadith are well-known gross errors. Al-Daaraqutni said about him, "Leader in the sunnah, lots of errors." Abu Ahmad al-Haakim said, "Perhaps he contradicts others in some of his hadith." It was already mentioned that ibn Adi followed up the mistakes he made, and that is the criterion for the statement concerning him [as to what hadith to accept and what to reject].4 Similarly, in Taqreeb al-Tahdheeb, which is basically a summary of his conclusions in Tahdheeb al-Tahdheeb, ibn Hajr described Nuaim with the words, Honest, makes lots of mistakes; jurist, most knowledgeable on laws of inheritance ... Ibn Adi followed up the hadith in which he made errors and the rest of his hadith are sound. 5 In his introduction to his commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari, ibn Hajr once again wrote about Nuaim, The great scholar (haa.fidh); al-Bukhaari met him but did not record anything from him in his Sahih except in one or two 1 Cf., ibn Muhammad, p. 224. 2 Cf., Muhammad Naasir al-Deen al-Albaani, footnotes to Amr ibn al-Aasim, Kitaab al-Sunnah (Beirut: al-Maktab al-Islaami, 1985), vol. 1 , pp. 12-13. 3 However, there is also some question about Uqbah ibn Aus, as shall be noted later. 4 Ahmad ibn Hajr, Tahdheeb al-Tahdheeb (Muassasat al-Risaalah), vol. 4, p. 236. 5 See ibn Hajr's statements in Maroof and al-Arnaoot, vol. 4, p. 2 1 . Hadith #41 : "None of You Truly Believes Until. .. " places, 1 and he recorded from him without stating the chains (mualaq) in other places. Muslim recorded from him in one place in the introduction to his work. The compilers of the Sunan works, save al-Nasaai, [also recorded from him]. Ahmad considered him trustworthy and said, "Nuaim was from the people of honesty, but he would be confused on some matters and would make errors concerning them." Al-Ajali called him trustworthy. Abu Haatim called him honest. Al-Nasaai called him weak. Abu Bishr al-Doolaabi stated that he had forged hadith. Ibn Adi commented on that, saying that al-Doolaabi was being extreme against him because [Nuaim] was extreme in his opposition to the scholars who used personal opinion (ahl al-ra'i). And that [ibn Adi's conclusion] is correct. And Allah knows best. 2 Finally, in Lisaan al-Meezaan, a work on criticized narrators, ibn Hajr only wrote the following related to Nuaim's status, "Ahmad, Yahya and al-Ajali considered him trustworthy."3 One sees that ibn Hajr was very consistent in all four of his works quoted above. However, when comparing what he wrote with what others wrote, one does get the feeling that he overlooked something or ignored some facts concerning Nuaim. For example, ibn Hajr's Lisaan al-Meezaan is an abridgment and critical edition of al-Dhahabi's Meezaan al-Itidaal. However, ibn Hajr deleted all of the following disparaging remarks concerning Nuaim that are found in al-Dhahabi's work: . . . One of the well-known leaders and scholars although he was weak (layin) in hadith ... Abu Dawood said, "Nuaim has about twenty hadith on the authority of the Prophet (peace be upon him) for which there is no source." Al-Nasaai said [about him], "He is weak." Al-Haafidh Abu Ali al-Naisaboori said, "I heard al-Nasaai mention Nuaim ibn Hammaad's virtues and his advancement in knowledge, understanding and the sunnah. I asked him about accepting his hadith and he said, 'The reports that he alone narrates when compared to the well-known leading scholars of hadith are many. He reached the point that one cannot use him as evidence."' ... Al-Azdi said, "Nuaim was one of the people who would fabricate hadith in order to strengthen the place of the sunnah as well as [fabricate] false attributions to defame al-Numan [Abu Hanifah]. All of those are lies."...
title_id: Hadis #41: 'Tidak Beriman Salah Seorang dari Kalian Hingga...' (Takhrij)
content_id:
Takhrij Hadis #41. Para ulama yang meriwayatkan hadis ini termasuk Ibn Abi Ashim dalam al-Sunnah, Ibn al-Jauzi dalam Dzamm al-Hawa, al-Baghawi dalam Syarh al-Sunnah dan Mashabih al-Sunnah, al-Khathib al-Baghdadi dalam Tarikh Baghdad, al-Dailami dalam al-Firdaus, Ibn Battah dalam al-Ibanah, dan Abu al-Qasim al-Asfahani dalam al-Hujjah. Hadis ini juga diriwayatkan oleh al-Hasan ibn Sufyan dalam al-Arba'in, al-Salafi dalam al-Arba'in al-Buldaniyyah, al-Harawi dalam Dzamm al-Kalam, dan Ibn Asakir dalam al-Arba'in.

Semua ulama tersebut meriwayatkan hadis ini melalui jalur Nu'aim ibn Hammad al-Khuza'i. Dalam kebanyakan kasus, jalurnya adalah: Nu'aim dari Abdul Wahhab ibn Abdul Majid al-Tsaqafi dari Hisyam ibn Hassan dari Muhammad ibn Sirin dari Uqbah ibn Aus dari Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-Ash. Selain Nu'aim, semua perawi tersebut adalah perawi yang pasti dapat diterima.

Penilaian hadis ini sebagian besar ditentukan oleh bagaimana seseorang memandang Nu'aim ibn Hammad. Di antara ulama yang membahas Nu'aim ibn Hammad, tampaknya Ibn Hajar memberikan penilaian tertinggi kepadanya. Dalam Tahdzib al-Tahdzib, Ibn Hajar menulis ringkasan pandangannya tentang Nu'aim: 'Adapun Nu'aim, kejujuran dan integritasnya terkonfirmasi, tetapi dalam hadis-hadisnya terdapat kesalahan besar yang terkenal. Al-Daraquthni berkata tentangnya: pemimpin dalam sunnah, banyak kesalahan. Abu Ahmad al-Hakim berkata: Mungkin beliau bertentangan dengan orang lain dalam beberapa hadisnya.' Dalam Taqrib al-Tahdzib, Ibn Hajar menggambarkan Nu'aim: 'Jujur, banyak membuat kesalahan; ahli fiqih, paling berpengetahuan tentang hukum waris... Ibn Adi menindaklanjuti hadis-hadis di mana beliau membuat kesalahan dan sisa hadisnya adalah sahih.'

Al-Imam Ahmad menganggapnya terpercaya dan berkata: 'Nu'aim adalah dari kalangan orang-orang jujur, tetapi beliau kadang bingung tentang beberapa hal dan membuat kesalahan mengenainya.' Al-Ajali menyebutnya terpercaya. Abu Hatim menyebutnya jujur. Al-Nasai menyebutnya lemah. Al-Azdi berkata bahwa Nu'aim adalah salah satu orang yang memalsukan hadis untuk memperkuat kedudukan sunnah. Ibn Adi mengomentari hal itu dengan mengatakan bahwa al-Daulabi bersikap berlebihan dalam menentangnya karena Nu'aim sangat keras menentang para ulama ahli ra'yi (pendapat). Wallahu a'lam.
content_ar: يُخرّج ابن رجب في هذا الفصل حديث «لا يؤمن أحدكم حتى يكون هواه تبعاً لما جئت به» الذي أورده النووي في الأربعين بوصفه الحديث الحادي والأربعين. ويذكر أن الحديث رواه ابن أبي عاصم في السنة، وابن الجوزي في ذم الهوى، والبغوي في شرح السنة والمصابيح، والخطيب البغدادي في التاريخ، والديلمي في الفردوس، وابن بطة في الإبانة، وأبو القاسم الأصبهاني في الحجة، وغيرهم. وقد رووه جميعاً بسند نعيم بن حماد الخزاعي عن عبد الوهاب بن عبد المجيد الثقفي عن هشام بن حسان عن محمد بن سيرين عن عقبة بن أوس عن عبد الله بن عمرو بن العاص. وسائر رجال السند سوى نعيم ثقات معروفون، غير أن الحكم على الحديث مرتبط بالحكم على نعيم بن حماد الذي اختُلف فيه اختلافاً كبيراً بين أئمة الجرح والتعديل.
